_meta.description: Maps synthetic identity anomaly types to MITRE ATT&CK Enterprise tactics/techniques
_meta.note: Fill in technique IDs by searching at https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/enterprise/

anomaly_mappings.description: User appears in two far-apart locations too quickly — credentials likely stolen
anomaly_mappings.notes: Attacker uses stolen creds from a different geo. Sub-technique depends on account type: T1078.002 (Domain Accounts) for AD, T1078.004 (Cloud Accounts) for SaaS.

anomaly_mappings.description: Login from a new country during user's typical sleeping hours
anomaly_mappings.notes: Combine T1078 (Valid Accounts) with geography/temporal context. Possible compromised credential resale. See also: DET0560 (Detection of Valid Account Abuse) analytic AN1546 for IdP geographic anomalies.

anomaly_mappings.description: Multiple rapid login failures indicating password guessing
anomaly_mappings.notes: T1110.003 (Password Spraying) fits best = one password tried across many accounts. Could also be T1110.001 (Guessing) if targeted. See analytic AN1276.

anomaly_mappings.description: First-seen device connecting via VPN — possible credential theft with anonymization
anomaly_mappings.notes: T1090.002 (External Proxy) covers VPN/anonymization services. Combine with T1078 (Valid Accounts) for stolen creds + VPN. See also: T1090.003 (Multi-hop Proxy) for layered anonymization.

anomaly_mappings.description: Large data transfer to/from a previously unseen IP — possible exfiltration
anomaly_mappings.notes: T1041 = data exfiltrated over existing C2 channel. Could also be T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service) if using cloud storage APIs. Size + new IP = strong C2/Exfil signal.

tactic_descriptions.Initial Access: The attacker is trying to get into your network.
tactic_descriptions.Credential Access: The attacker is trying to steal account names and passwords.
tactic_descriptions.Exfiltration: The attacker is trying to steal data.
tactic_descriptions.Command and Control: The attacker is communicating with compromised systems to control them.
